title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
quotes:
Three distinct states of PECAM1(CD31)-expressing vascular endothelial cells (VE1,
VE2, VE3) are present in adult dermis. VE3, which forms ~2% of endothelial cells, is
characterized by gamma synuclein (SNCG) and high expression of the venular capillary
marker ACKR1

VE3 co-express inflammatory cytokines,
chemokines and leukocyte adhesion molecules including IL6, IL33, SELE and ICAM1

we performed
whole-mount immunostaining of healthy dermis and identified SNCG+PECAM1+(VE3)
distended vascular structures in the superficial dermis

these cells
may be post-capillary venular cells regulating leukocyte adhesion and migration

we observed significant expansion of VE3 in AD and psoriasis lesional
skin

Fetal skin VE cells aligned transcriptionally with adult skin
VE3 and also express genes involved in leukocyte adhesion and trafficking

Endothelial cells in the healthy adult dermis comprise vascular endothelium (PECAM1,
EMCN) and lymphatic endothelium (LYVE1, PDPN)
summary: Reynolds et al. (2021) identify three PECAM1+ vascular endothelial states in adult dermis. VE3 is the minor state (~2% of ECs), marked by SNCG and high ACKR1, co-expressing inflammatory cytokines (IL6, IL33) and leukocyte adhesion molecules (SELE, ICAM1) similar to lymph node HEVs. Whole-mount immunostaining validates SNCG+PECAM1+ distended structures in superficial dermis. VE3 expands in AD and psoriasis and aligns transcriptionally with fetal VE.

title: Single-Cell Transcriptional Analysis Deciphers the Inflammatory Response of Skin-Resident Stromal Cells
quotes: Reynolds et al. reported that some vascular endothelial cells, which expressed γ-synuclein (SNCG) and high level of ACKR1 (the venular capillary marker), might function as postcapillary venular cells. They found that ACKR1+ endothelial cells and CXCL8 (IL8)+ macrophages interacted with each other by cell communication analysis and further confirmed this finding with immunofluorescence staining in situ

ACKR1+ endothelial cells coexpressed inflammatory cytokines, chemokines, and leukocyte adhesion molecules including IL6, IL33, SELE, and ICAM1, implying that they might regulate leukocyte adhesion and migration
summary: Liu et al. (2022) summarise VE3 as ACKR1+ post-capillary venular endothelial cells that co-express IL6, IL33, SELE and ICAM1 and interact with CXCL8+ macrophages, supporting a role in regulating leukocyte adhesion and migration.

title: Novel Blood Vascular Endothelial Subtype-Specific Markers in Human Skin Unearthed by Single-Cell Transcriptomic Profiling
quotes: Post-capillary venules represent the primary sites for leukocyte trafficking and subsequently the extravasation into peripheral tissues. The characterization of the atypical chemokine receptor ACKR1 has favored the identification of intact venous vasculature in multiple murine tissues
ACKR1 also represents an important molecule that distinguished the downstream venular compartments from arterioles and post-arterial capillaries in human skin
summary: He et al. (2022) independently characterize ACKR1 as the key marker of post-capillary venular endothelium in human skin, consistent with VE3 identity. Post-capillary venules are the primary sites for leukocyte trafficking and extravasation.
